mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market and healthy consumer spending capacity, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the annualized 3-month change in the Consumer Price Index exceeds a 5% threshold, it signals accelerating inflation that prompts central bank tightening, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If, when the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales and low unemployment indicates robust consumer confidence and purchasing power, driving corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major GDP component
failure_condition: If periods of simultaneously rising RSAFS and declining UNRATE do not correlate with positive S&P 500 20-day returns, or the correlation turns negative over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, sustained increases in the federal funds rate raise the cost of capital for businesses and reduce the present value of future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed signals potential rate hikes in 2022
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 consistently generates positive 60-day returns during periods where FEDFUNDS is actively increasing, or if the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts below a critical threshold signals a contraction in the housing market, which often precedes broader economic slowdowns and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon of one week.
evidence_refs: fact:housing is a cyclical economic indicator
failure_condition: If, after the trigger, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of cases, or the average return is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side activity, indicating robust economic conditions that support corporate profits and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial output correlates with economic health
failure_condition: If periods of positive INDPRO growth are consistently associated with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns, or the Rank IC falls below 0.05 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment and high inflation creates an overheating economy scenario, forcing aggressive monetary policy tightening that significantly increases recession risk and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If, when both trigger conditions are met, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are predominantly positive, or the average return is not significantly negative (p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in real GDP signals a recessionary environment, where corporate earnings decline due to reduced demand and increased uncertainty, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon of one week.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic activity
failure_condition: If during periods of declining GDPC1, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of observations, or the average return is not significantly negative.
